page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2157.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2979.0. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 34.18 GB/sec, and bzip3 decompression processes at 14.25 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 8669.32 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8369.0. Memory read-only bandwidth for cached small blocks reaches 641.58 GB/sec, while uncached large block reads operate at 75.84 GB/sec.

The instance exhibits certain performance limitations, particularly in medium-sized language model text generation, where the 7B model text generation speed drops to a weak 8.5 tokens/sec. Additionally, network bandwidth is constrained to a baseline of 2 Gbps, and there is no local storage, requiring external network-attached storage. This hardware profile is suited for compute-bound tasks that benefit from advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, such as text processing, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight single-threaded data compression.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2157.0 and top-tier text processing performance. However, multi-core benchmarks and medium-model LLM text generation are average to weak, and network bandwidth is limited to a baseline of 2 Gbps. This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as text processing, software compilation, and static web serving where high memory-to-CPU ratios and local storage are not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for single-threaded and text-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM.
Strong single-threaded performance in text processing and compression benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware configuration.
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and static web serving.
